disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid onset over about 4 hours of severe, diffuse, symmetrical muscle aching with subjective fever and malaise, in a previously well person
trigger_question_examples: Tell me what's been happening today.
Where is the aching and how quickly did it come on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was fine this morning and by this afternoon my whole body was aching — deep, all over, both sides the same — and I started feeling hot and wiped out. It's got worse over about four hours.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recently started antihypertensive — a new regular tablet begun roughly 8 weeks ago
trigger_question_examples: Have you started any new medications recently?
What tablets do you take for your blood pressure, and when did you start them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I take hydralazine, 50 mg three times a day for my blood pressure. I started it about two months ago — the doctor added it because my pressure was still up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Absence of localising infective symptoms — no cough, coryza, sore throat, dysuria, focal pain, diarrhoea or recent travel
trigger_question_examples: Any cough, sore throat, runny nose, or burning when you pass water?
Any tummy pain, diarrhoea, or recent travel?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — no cough or cold, no sore throat, no burning when I go to the toilet, no tummy trouble, and I haven't been travelling.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Mild joint aching without described swelling; no skin rash visible to the patient
trigger_question_examples: Are any of your joints swollen or red?
Have you noticed any rash on your skin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My joints ache a little but I can't see any swelling. I've looked and I don't have any rash anywhere.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute functional decline with difficulty mobilising in a person who lives alone — falls concern
trigger_question_examples: Are you managing to move around safely at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can barely get out of the chair and I'm on my own. I'm frightened I'll fall trying to get to the bathroom.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain, breathlessness, or spreading rash at presentation (pertinent negatives that would signal serositis or a severe cutaneous reaction)
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain or trouble breathing?
Is any rash spreading or blistering?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain and my breathing's okay. There's no rash spreading or blistering.
